title: First Solar Reaffirms 2026 Guidance as CURE Launch and Record India Sales Drive Q1 Margin Expansion
text: First Solar delivered record first-quarter 2026 revenue, record sales in India, meaningful margin expansion, and adjusted EBITDA above the top end of first quarter preview range. India has emerged as a high-volume engine, accounting for 1 GW of sales this quarter at approximately 20 cents per watt, utilizing the company's vertically integrated domestic capacity in that market. Operating margin improved to 33.1%, up from 26.2% in the first quarter of the previous year. Net income for the quarter stood at $347 million, or $3.22 per diluted share, up from $210 million in Q1 2025, while adjusted EBITDA rose to $520 million from $379 million in the previous year's first quarter. The 2026 guidance framework assumes the India facility operates at near full capacity to serve a robust domestic book-and-bill market. The India business has been underpinned by strong sales in the PMKUSUM segment, where the firm's compliance with DCR requirements makes it an attractive option for developers. The CURE technology launch represents a significant achievement for the company, with Series 6 lines now ramping at Perrysburg. This technology is expected to unlock substantial additional revenue through technology adjusters embedded in the backlog, with most benefits expected in 2027-2028.
